case_name: 5.	It is not in dispute that under Polish law the claimant has a direct action, nominally in tort, against the third defendant in respect of liabilities which may arise in respect of their insured, the second defendant.  As explained by the claimant this is an "Odenbreit claim" namely an action brought by a claimant against a wrongdoer's EU insurer in the Courts of the EU Member State where the claimant is domiciled.  It is named afterOdenbreit v FBTO Schadeverzekeringen NVCase C-463/06 (decided by reference to section 3 of the Brussels I Regulation: Council Regulation 44/2001 now recast in: Regulation No 2015/2012).  It is an action in tort (Maher & Another v Groupama Grand Est[2010] 1 WLR 1564(CA)), and, subject to the applicable law of the tort and the existence of a direct right of action against an insurer, by section 3 of the recast Brussels I Regulation it gives a claimant a right to assert the jurisdiction of the English Court: the Court of the claimant's domicile (seeOdenbreitabove).

evidence_index.text: This is a claim for damages for personal injury sustained during cosmetic surgery undergone by the claimant Ms Laura Clarke on 7 January 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Ms Clarke who was born on 11 December 1985 claims against Mr Adam Kaleciński, the surgeon who performed the breast and thigh procedures upon Ms Clarke, in Poland.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The real issue in the case is as to liability.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant sues both the surgeon and the clinic, both in contract and in tort.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The claimant's skeleton argument stated it was common ground that Polish law would apply to the claimant's claim in tort.
evidence_index.speaker: Claimant's legal argument

evidence_index.text: The expert outlined how liability for negligence was decided in the Polish law, referring to Article 415 of the Civil.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Mr Urso-Baiarda further observes, having studied the website entry which induced Laura Clarke to have surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: I have come to the clear conclusion the claimant has proved her case in tort as well as in contract.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The standard of care outlined as necessary by the Polish law expert is amply fulfilled by the terms of the report from Mr Urso-Baiarda.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I have no hesitation in concluding that the case of negligence is made out against both the first and second defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The care offered by the clinic likewise.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The third defendant is liable to the extent of the indemnity.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Post-operative pain and fever following cosmetic surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent breast augmentation and thigh liposuction in Poland. Post-operatively, she experienced severe pain, fever, and shivering.

vital_signs.heart_rate: 136 beats per minute upon arrival in UK
vital_signs.temperature: 39.5 degrees Celsius upon arrival in UK

focused_exam.general: Patient appeared frail and in distress

requestables.name: Blood Culture
requestables.description: To identify the causative organism of the infection

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: To assess the extent of infection and abscess formation

reveal.detailed_findings: Imaging performed but findings not detailed in judgment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe post-operative symptoms

key_events.event: Patient arrives at UK hospital with severe symptoms
Patient experiences severe pain and fever
Patient returns to clinic with worsening symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presenting with severe post-operative symptoms
decision_points.prompt: What should be the immediate management for a patient with suspected severe post-operative infection?

options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent intervention required to control infection

options.description: Continue with oral antibiotics and observe

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued with oral antibiotics and observation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed symptoms were normal post-operative course

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated urgent surgical intervention was required

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to recognize and treat severe infection promptly

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered from severe infections post-surgery

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to implement adequate infection control measures
Causation established - infections were preventable with proper care

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to implement adequate infection control measures
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper measures would have prevented infections

factual_timeline.event: Patient undergoes cosmetic surgery in Poland
Patient arrives at UK hospital with severe symptoms

allegations.allegation: Failure to implement adequate infection control measures
